label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user provided a passage about the decline of the Sacramento River salmon run and is asking which of the four statements is true based on the passage. First, I need to go through each option and check against the passage. Starting with option (A): "Last fall, there was the second lowest number of grown salmon returning on record." The passage says that last fall about 90,000 adult salmon returned, which is the second lowest number on record. So this matches. The term "grown" here is probably meant to say "adult," which the passage uses. So (A) seems correct. Option (B): "The salmon migrates to the ocean when they grow up." The passage states that wild salmon are born in streams, migrate to the ocean when they're juveniles. "Juveniles" means young, not when they grow up. So (B) is incorrect. Option (C): "The farm-raised salmon is not as delicious and nutrients as the wild salmon." The passage mentions that there will be abundant supplies of farm-raised salmon, but it doesn't compare their taste or nutritional value to wild salmon. So (C) is not supported by the text.
